description: default target = x86_64-unknown-linux-musl (static, no glibc dynamic loader)

asi.build_changes: .cargo/config.toml pins target = x86_64-unknown-linux-musl; autoresearch.sh mirrors target/<triple>/release/pi to target/release/pi for tooling.
asi.finding: Switching default target to x86_64-unknown-linux-musl removes glibc dynamic-loader / PLT cost. Startup 1785→680 µs (-62%). Size 5029→5159 KiB (+2.6%, the static musl libc is small). Net score 6814→5839 (-14.3%, real — 5/5 trials are 656-697 µs, well outside any prior noise band).
asi.watch_outs: If anyone profiles with perf/heaptrack later they may want PI_AR_TARGET=x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu or `cargo build --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`.

description: non-PIE static reloc + post-link strip of .eh_frame/.gcc_except_table (panic=abort already)

asi.finding: Stacked two safe wins on top of the musl base: (1) `relocation-model=static` via .cargo/config.toml [target.x86_64-unknown-linux-musl].rustflags — emits a non-PIE ELF, removes `.rela.dyn` (~312 KiB) and the dynamic-loader reloc-apply pass; (2) post-link `objcopy --remove-section=.eh_frame{,_hdr} --remove-section=.gcc_except_table` in autoresearch.sh and `make build-release` — with `panic=abort` these tables are dead weight (~286 KiB).
asi.remaining_levers: `-Z build-std` to recompile std with panic_abort would shave more eh_frame from libstd, but needs nightly. `cargo bloat --crates` would tell us where the .text 3.0 MiB still lives — reqwest/rustls/clap likely top-3.
asi.verdict: score 5839 → 5303 (-9.2%, real — size delta is deterministic, startup nearly equal). Keep.
